parentQuestion: A 45-year-old patient presents with inability to feel pain or temperature on the...

scaffolds.q: Sensory pathways decussate (cross over) at different levels depending on the type of sensation.
scaffolds.explain: Different sensory modalities cross at different anatomical locations. The spinothalamic tract (pain/temperature) decussates at the spinal level. The dorsal column pathway (proprioception/fine touch) decussates in the medulla. This creates distinct patterns of sensory loss depending on lesion location.

scaffolds.q: The spinothalamic tract carries pain and temperature sensations.
scaffolds.explain: The spinothalamic tract is the ascending pathway specifically dedicated to pain and temperature sensation. First-order neurons synapse in the dorsal horn, second-order neurons decussate immediately and ascend in the lateral column to the thalamus.
